title: nicotiana angustifolia var. crispa
intro: Nicotiana angustifolia var. crispa, also known as the narrowleaf tobacco, is a type of flowering plant in the nightshade family, native to the Great Plains of North America. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its ability to relieve coughs and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Nicotiana angustifolia var. crispa include its potential to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
